tests.test_body:
define i31 @load_with_non_power_of_2_element_type(ptr %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @load_with_non_power_of_2_element_type(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[LV:%.*]] = load <4 x i31>, ptr [[X:%.*]], align 16
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[R:%.*]] = extractelement <4 x i31> [[LV]], i32 1
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i31 [[R]]
  %lv = load <4 x i31>, ptr %x, align 16
  %r = extractelement <4 x i31> %lv, i32 1
  ret i31 %r
}

define i1 @load_with_non_power_of_2_element_type_2(ptr %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @load_with_non_power_of_2_element_type_2(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[LV:%.*]] = load <8 x i1>, ptr [[X:%.*]], align 1
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[R:%.*]] = extractelement <8 x i1> [[LV]], i32 1
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[R]]
  %lv = load <8 x i1>, ptr %x, align 1
  %r = extractelement <8 x i1> %lv, i32 1
  ret i1 %r
}

define void @insert_store_v32i1(ptr %p) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @insert_store_v32i1(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[VEC:%.*]] = load <32 x i1>, ptr [[P:%.*]], align 4
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[INS:%.*]] = insertelement <32 x i1> [[VEC]], i1 true, i64 0
; CHECK-NEXT:    store <32 x i1> [[INS]], ptr [[P]], align 4
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret void
  %vec = load <32 x i1>, ptr %p, align 4
  %ins = insertelement <32 x i1> %vec, i1 true, i64 0
  store <32 x i1> %ins, ptr %p, align 4
  ret void
}

issue.title: miscompile of store to element of <i1 x N>
issue.author: zygoloid

comments.author: zygoloid
comments.body:
Storing to `v32b[1]` gives this, which doesn't look like it should be valid IR to me:
```llvm
  store i1 %b, ptr getelementptr inbounds (<32 x i1>, ptr @v32b, i64 0, i64 1), align 1
```
(This is forming a non-byte-aligned pointer...)

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Problematic pass is VectorCombine: https://llvm.godbolt.org/z/s6KcEqTcv Doesn't deal with non-byte-sized types correctly.
